text.title: Domain Interactions and Assessment Rules
text.summary: Domain interaction matrix showing upstream failure cascades, routing rules, and antipatterns for domain-based assessment.

section.title: Domain Interaction Matrix
section.p: When a domain struggles, these others are typically affected:

list.item: Stakeholder weak → Team, Delivery, Measurement suffer (misaligned expectations propagate)
Team weak → Project Work, Delivery suffer (execution degrades)
Development Approach wrong → Planning, Project Work, Delivery suffer (wrong tools for the work)
Planning weak → Project Work, Delivery, Measurement suffer (no baseline to measure against)
Project Work weak → Delivery, Measurement suffer (output quality degrades)
Delivery weak → Stakeholder, Measurement suffer (value not visible)
Measurement weak → all domains suffer (no visibility into what is wrong)
Uncertainty weak → all domains suffer (unmanaged risks materialise unpredictably)

rule.statement: Re-assess domains at every project gate (kickoff, mid-point, pre-launch, close), not weekly; weekly drives status theatre.
rule.rationale: Domain assessment is a strategic tool; overuse dilutes its signal and creates reporting overhead without changing PM decisions.

rule.statement: Start every status report with the two weakest domains; do not rotate through all 8.
rule.rationale: Focus on the weakest domains drives action; rotating through all eight creates the illusion of coverage without improving anything.

rule.statement: The Uncertainty domain requires an active risk register to score green; absence of a register means automatic red.
rule.rationale: Uncertainty is most often under-resourced; requiring a concrete artefact prevents superficial compliance.

rule.statement: Domains are NOT phases; never write "Stakeholder phase complete" or "Team phase done".
rule.rationale: Domains are ongoing concurrent concerns that persist for the entire project; "complete" misrepresents the nature of domain management.

rule.statement: When dropping a domain for a low-stakes project, drop the entire domain, not partial methodologies; "light EVM" is usually fake EVM.
rule.rationale: Half-applied methodologies consume overhead without delivering the discipline benefits; commit or skip explicitly.

antipattern.description: Collapsing the 8 domains back into the old 5 process groups (Initiate/Plan/Execute/M&C/Close).
antipattern.reason: This is a PMBoK 6 regression; domains are outcome-based auditing primitives, not sequential phases.

antipattern.description: Asking an LLM "is this domain healthy?" without providing evidence artefacts or metrics.
antipattern.reason: Without inputs, the agent produces generic prose that does not reflect actual project health; always feed evidence and require citations.
